completion_criteria_checked: All three scripts push the current branch to origin automatically after a successful -Commit, branch detected dynamically. -- MET: read the identical diff in all three scripts directly; git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD used in each, no hardcoded branch name anywhere.

-Commit's existing add/commit behavior unchanged; only a push step appended. -- MET: diff shows the git add -A / git commit block is byte-identical to before in all three files; only new lines were inserted after the existing commit success path.

Push failure reported clearly without destructive failure; commit not undone. -- MET: confirmed by reading the code (Write-Output, not Fail, on non-zero push exit) AND by independently re-testing myself in a fresh scratch git repo forcing a real push scenario.

Push only attempted after -Commit succeeds; no-Commit runs never push. -- MET: the push block is entirely inside the existing if ($Commit) branch in all three files; the else branch contains no push logic.

No push to any branch/remote other than current branch and origin; no force-push. -- MET: grep-equivalent read of all three files confirms only 'git push origin $currentBranch' appears, no -f/--force flag, no other remote name.

New decision recorded, explicitly superseding DECISION-020's push clause. -- MET: DECISION-065 present; DECISION-020's own Status line was updated to note the partial supersession, matching the precedent set for DECISION-019.

Functionally tested, not read-through only. -- MET AND INDEPENDENTLY REPEATED: re-tested the exact verbatim push logic myself in a separate, freshly created scratch git repository (with its own local bare fake remote), this time on a 'main'-named branch (the worker's own test happened to use 'master'), confirming the logic generalizes across branch names rather than being coincidentally correct for one name.

No change to verdicts/classes/Acceptance Boundary Rules/fallback/other scripts/schemas. -- MET: git diff --stat confirms only the three targeted scripts, three docs, and expected live state/handoff/result files changed; no schema or other script touched.
docs/HANDOFF_PACKET_SPEC.md and docs/REPO_GOVERNANCE.md updated to reflect new default. -- MET: both passages read directly, accurately describe the new behavior.

out_of_scope_findings: None found. git diff --stat confirms only scripts/close-out-verified-task.ps1, scripts/return-inadequate-work.ps1, scripts/archive-held-cycle.ps1, docs/DECISIONS.md, docs/HANDOFF_PACKET_SPEC.md, docs/REPO_GOVERNANCE.md, and the expected live state/handoff/result files changed. risks: Self-verified under DECISION-033 degraded fallback. No independent second-party (Codex) review has occurred. GPT secondary review not yet performed on this specific cycle. This is a meaningful governance/authority change (superseding DECISION-020's push clause) verified only under the fallback -- the owner's own direct instruction is the actual authority here, not this verification, which only checks that the implementation matches that instruction correctly.

Verification Depth Policy row applied: Class B, truth-surface-affecting -> strict. Applied via full diff review of all three scripts plus an independent, separately-constructed functional re-test (different branch name than the worker used) rather than trusting the worker's test transcript alone.

Neither the worker's nor my own testing exercised the real repo's actual GitHub remote -- both used scratch/local git repositories by design, to avoid any risk to real history. The very first real push under this new behavior (this task's own close-out) will be the first true end-to-end confirmation against the actual remote.

A failed push is currently only visible in script output; nothing alerts the owner if a push silently fails and is never retried. Disclosed by the worker as a known, un-addressed gap, not fixed by this task.
summary: Verified pcc-brr5-003 (Safety Net: Automatic Push On Commit) at 'strict' depth. Read identical diffs across all three targeted scripts confirming the push addition is scoped exactly as required (inside the existing -Commit branch only, dynamic branch detection, non-fatal failure handling, no force-push or alternate remote). Independently re-tested the exact push logic myself in a separate fresh scratch git repository against a real local bare remote, deliberately using a different branch name ('main') than the worker's own test ('master') to confirm the logic is genuinely branch-name-agnostic rather than coincidentally correct. All nine completion criteria are met with direct, independently-reproduced evidence. This is a real, owner-directed governance change (superseding DECISION-020's per-time push approval) -- the change itself was directly authorized by the owner in this conversation; this verification confirms the implementation correctly matches that authorization, not that the authorization itself needed verifying.
next_action: Task 'pcc-brr5-003' is complete and verified PASS. Close out via scripts/close-out-verified-task.ps1 -Commit -- this will be the first real, live use of the new auto-push behavior, so it also serves as the true end-to-end confirmation against the actual GitHub remote. No further owner action is needed to push this specific cycle; the new behavior handles it automatically per the owner's own instruction.
